summary.notes: 12 files under cuda_native/native/ and cuda_native/src/native/ are byte-identical copies of their parent. build/artifacts/ and training/build/artifacts/ are hexa compiler output (Generated by HEXA self-host compiler). The real C dependency surface is 7 hand-written files.

hand_written_source.description: Human-authored C code that hexa depends on via FFI or #include

compiler_generated.description: C files emitted by hexa self-host compiler (// Generated by HEXA self-host compiler). Source truth is the .hexa file; these are intermediate build products.

build_artifacts.description: C files in build/artifacts/ — hexa compiler output, NOT source truth. Generated by `hexa cc` from .hexa sources.
build_artifacts.note: All begin with '// Generated by HEXA self-host compiler' and #include runtime.c. These are ephemeral compilation products — not true C dependencies.

duplicate_copies.description: Byte-identical copies within cuda_native/ directory tree

files.description: HEXA C Runtime Library — tagged values (HexaVal), strings, arrays, maps, GC, string interning, inline caches. The foundation all compiler-generated .c files #include. Provides hexa_int/hexa_float/hexa_str/hexa_array_*/hexa_map_* primitives.
calls: libc (stdio, stdlib, string, math, dlfcn, time, unistd)
files.notes: Core of hexa self-hosting. This IS the hexa runtime — replacing it means the compiler must emit native code directly instead of C. Blocked until hexa has a native backend (LLVM/cranelift). ~3676 lines of tagged-value dispatch, GC, hash maps, inline caches. THE hardest piece to eliminate.

files.description: Hot-path tensor pointer r/w kernels (f32/f64/i32 ptr_read/ptr_write, struct pack). Explicitly marked @hot_kernel DO NOT MIGRATE TO HEXA — HexaVal wrap/dispatch per element would cause 10-100x slowdown.
files.notes: Performance-critical raw pointer operations. Moving to hexa requires hexa's type system to support unboxed f32/i32 types with zero-overhead memory access. Blocked on hexa compiler unboxed-type support. Intentionally kept in C per project policy.

files.description: Skeleton stub for @gpu codegen backend. NO real GPU emission — every function returns a placeholder. Locks in C ABI between hexa_cc.c and future GPU backend.
files.notes: Research scaffold only. Will be replaced by real GPU codegen when rt#45 wave-6 lands. Could be rewritten in hexa once the GPU backend contract is finalized, but low priority since it's a stub.

files.description: Complete transformer training step: forward pass (embed -> N layers -> LM head -> CE loss), backward pass (full gradient computation), AdamW optimizer. Uses cuBLAS for matmuls, host-side helpers for layernorm/softmax/silu/CE. Single hot-path replacing ~200k hexa interpreter statements.
files.notes: Critical training infrastructure. Has CPU stub mode for mac dev. The actual CUDA matmul hot path MUST stay native (cuBLAS). However, host-side helpers (layernorm, softmax, silu, CE loss) are candidates for hexa replacement once hexa supports unboxed float arrays. Makefile builds libtrain_step.so/stub.

files.description: Flat FFI wrapper: packs hexa scalars (int/long/float) into C structs for train_step(). Global FfiState with ModelConfig, weights, grads, adam, batch. Provides train_ffi_init/step/cleanup entry points.
files.notes: Pure marshaling layer. Could be eliminated if hexa gains struct-passing FFI support (currently can only pass scalars). The struct-packing boilerplate is the main reason this exists.

files.description: BLAS/OpenMP ABI shim — bridges hexa FFI type mismatch (hexa float->C double) to real cblas_sgemm/sdot/saxpy/sscal float ABI on ARM64. Also provides vectorized eltwise ops: silu_fwd/bwd, rmsnorm_fwd/bwd, attn_softmax_causal, cross_entropy, embed_lookup/scatter, zero/copy/sgd.
files.notes: EXISTS ONLY because hexa compiler emits double for float FFI params (agent #18 fix in flight). Once hexa_v2 emits correct float FFI typedefs, the BLAS shims (sgemm/sdot/saxpy/sscal) can be replaced with direct extern fn declarations. The eltwise ops (silu, rmsnorm, softmax, CE) are auto-vectorized C that would need hexa SIMD intrinsics to match performance. 1400x faster than hexa linked-list matmul at 256^2.

files.description: Apple vDSP ABI shim — provides vDSP-accelerated rmsnorm_fwd/bwd, softmax_row, swiglu_fwd/bwd for Mac ARM64 training. Uses vDSP_svesq, vDSP_vmul, vvexpf, vvrecf for SIMD vectorization.
calls: Apple Accelerate/vDSP (vDSP_svesq, vDSP_vmul, vDSP_vsmul, vDSP_vsadd, vvexpf, vvrecf, vDSP_dotpr, vDSP_vadd, vDSP_vsub, vDSP_maxv, vDSP_sve)
files.notes: Mac-only (Accelerate framework). Same hexa float->double FFI workaround as hxblas. Direct vDSP calls need the ABI shim. Replacing requires either: (a) fix hexa FFI float ABI so extern fn can call vDSP directly, or (b) implement equivalent SIMD in hexa (no current path). Lower priority than hxblas since it's platform-specific.

files.description: Compiler-generated CLM training loop with knowledge-retention (KR) evaluation. Hexa source compiled to C via hexa self-host compiler.
files.notes: COMPILER OUTPUT — not a real C dependency. Source truth is the .hexa file. This .c is an intermediate artifact of the hexa->C compilation pipeline. Will disappear when hexa gains native codegen.

files.description: Compiler-generated hexa self-host compiler (hexa_cc). Full lexer+parser+type_checker+codegen in a single file. Generated from hexa source.
files.notes: COMPILER OUTPUT. The hexa compiler compiles itself to C, which is then compiled with cc/gcc to produce the hexa binary. This is the self-hosting bootstrap chain. Eliminating this requires an LLVM/native backend for hexa.

files.description: Compiler-generated C codegen module (v2). Emits HexaVal-based C code from AST.
files.notes: COMPILER OUTPUT. Source truth is codegen_c2.hexa.

files.description: Compiler-generated hexa tokenizer/lexer. Keyword detection, identifier parsing, token construction.
Compiler-generated hexa parser. Recursive descent parser producing AST nodes.
Compiler-generated hexa type checker. Scope management, struct/enum registry, generic type validation.
Byte-identical copy of training/hxblas_wrapper.c deployed into cuda_native.
files.notes: DUPLICATE of training/hxblas_wrapper.c. Should be deduplicated.

build_artifacts_summary.description: All files in build/artifacts/, training/build/artifacts/, anima-agent/build/artifacts/ are compiler-generated C. They all begin with '// Generated by HEXA self-host compiler' and #include runtime.c. These are NOT C dependencies — they are the OUTPUT of hexa->C compilation.

makefiles.description: Builds libtrain_step.so (cuda) or libtrain_step_stub.so (CPU stub). Three modes: stub (mac), cuda (H100), test.
FPGA bitstream build flow (yosys + nextpnr-ice40). Drives Verilog/HDL toolchain — no C compilation.
makefiles.note: Not a C dependency — Verilog only.

build_scripts.description: Hexa build script that assembles a bash runner to invoke nvcc/gcc for compiling libtrain_native.so. Replaces previous build.sh.
build_scripts.note: This is the main C compilation trigger for the training native path.

legacy_shell_scripts.description: Archived legacy launcher (Python era). References make/gcc.

phase1_easy.description: Compiler output cleanup — these .c files are already generated from .hexa source. Action: add to .gitignore or document as build artifacts.
phase1_easy.action: Gitignore build/artifacts/ dirs. Deduplicate cuda_native/native/ vs cuda_native/src/native/ copies. These are not true C dependencies — they are hexa compiler intermediates.

phase2_medium.description: FFI ABI shims — removable once hexa compiler emits correct float FFI typedefs (agent #18 fix).
blockers: hexa_v2 float FFI ABI fix (agent #18) — once hexa correctly maps Float to C float instead of double, the BLAS shims (sgemm/sdot/saxpy/sscal) become direct extern fn declarations
hexa struct-passing FFI — train_ffi.c exists because hexa can only pass scalars, not C structs
phase2_medium.action: Land hexa_v2 FFI fix. Then replace hxblas_wrapper BLAS shims with direct hexa extern fn. Rewrite train_ffi.c struct packing in hexa with raw pointer packing (partially done in train_step_ffi.hexa).
phase2_medium.estimated_effort: 1-2 weeks after hexa_v2 FFI fix lands

phase3_hard.description: Core runtime and performance-critical kernels — the fundamental C dependency surface.
blockers: runtime.c: Hexa must gain a native codegen backend (LLVM/cranelift) that emits machine code directly instead of C. This eliminates the entire HexaVal tagged-value runtime, string interning, GC, hash maps, inline caches — all 3676 lines.
tensor_kernels.c: Hexa must support unboxed float/int types with zero-overhead raw pointer access. Currently explicitly marked @hot_kernel DO NOT MIGRATE.
train_step.c: cuBLAS/CUDA FFI calls must stay native. Host-side helpers (layernorm, softmax, silu, CE) could migrate once hexa has unboxed float arrays.
hxvdsp_wrapper.c: Requires hexa SIMD intrinsics or direct vDSP FFI with correct float ABI.
phase3_hard.action: Implement hexa native backend (replaces runtime.c + all compiler-generated C). Add unboxed numeric types to hexa (replaces tensor_kernels.c). Keep cuBLAS/CUDA calls as extern fn FFI (train_step.c matmul core stays native).
phase3_hard.estimated_effort: 6-12 months (native backend is a compiler engineering project)

dependency_chain.compilation: hexa compiler (itself compiled from hexa via self-hosting)

key_findings: The REAL C dependency surface is only 7 hand-written files totaling ~6,000 lines. Everything else is hexa compiler output.
53 of the 65+ .c files found are compiler-generated intermediates ('Generated by HEXA self-host compiler'). These are NOT C dependencies — they are hexa build artifacts.
12 files are byte-identical duplicates within the cuda_native directory tree (native/ vs src/native/ copies).
The #1 blocker for full self-hosting is runtime.c (3,676 lines) — it IS the hexa runtime. Eliminating it requires a native codegen backend.
The #2 blocker is the hexa FFI float ABI bug (hexa Float -> C double instead of float). Agent #18 fix would eliminate hxblas_wrapper.c and hxvdsp_wrapper.c.
train_step.c cuBLAS calls will likely ALWAYS need a C/CUDA bridge — this is a natural FFI boundary, not a self-hosting deficiency.
The Makefile for FPGA (anima-physics/fpga/) is Verilog toolchain only — zero C dependency.
Build artifact .c files should be added to .gitignore to reduce confusion about what is source vs generated.
